title: Foundation Brain Migration v1 — Source Gap Audit Blocker Ledger (vs SIASIU Path A)
authority: SIASIU Path A live brain (server.py/api/chat→brain.chat→ssbrain.engine) 기준 authoritative
purpose: control(foundation-control)이 자기 해석으로 재구현하지 못하도록 Path A 기준 blocker 확정
verdict_definition: Foundation이 Path A를 실제 이식한 것이 아니라 비슷한 구조를 새로 만든 상태 → 완료 선언 불가
v1_completion_blockers: MAND-16: Foundation core에 K-beauty domain data 하드코딩
MAND-17: golden test가 사고흐름 아닌 field만 검증

mandated_18_findings.evidence: 원본 '★휴리스틱 ❌ LLM이 의미로 분류' (brain.py:706) → core.py:677-678 any(k in n for k in _GR_INJECTION) 키워드 매칭
원본 user/episode/memory_fact/fact_type_registry 테이블(83-85). Foundation은 session dict만(983-995). '나 레티놀 알레르기'를 다음 대화에 못 기억
Foundation에 추출 메커니즘 완전 부재 — 대화에서 영속 사실 추출 0
avoid_ingredient/allergy/pregnancy_nursing 불변 보장 부재 — 안전 비대칭 메모리 없음

mandated_18_findings.required_action: verify_output 모든 호출에 사용자 avoid_atoms 실배선
mandated_18_findings.evidence: core.py verify_output(avoid_atoms=None) 시그니처는 있으나 호출 3곳(840·1021·1031)에서 한 번도 안 받음 → avoid_violation 검사 inert
원본 DeepSeek가 답을 생성(사고). v1 SYS '주어진 key_points·evidence만 사용·새 정보 만들지마'(20-26) → 결정은 규칙(_classify_intent/_compose_answer)이 전담
composer SYS는 축약본 — 상담 페르소나 상실
cross-encoder rerank·RRF 다채널 융합·CLIR 다국어 확장 부재

mandated_18_findings.required_action: regex → LLM 의미검증 복원(의미 위반 누락 방지)
mandated_18_findings.evidence: 원본 LLM 0-temp 검수(internal/efficacy/avoid). Foundation regex 패턴(_VERIFY_LEAK/_VERIFY_OVERCLAIM) — 의미 위반 누락
원본 위반 시 LLM 교정 재생성→실패 시 안전문구. Foundation answer_text=None→rule_compose(템플릿)

mandated_18_findings.category: Foundation core에 K-beauty domain data 하드코딩
mandated_18_findings.required_action: 도메인 데이터 전부 profiles.py 주입화(framework/data 분리)

mandated_18_findings.category: golden test가 사고흐름 아닌 field만 검증
mandated_18_findings.evidence: 20케이스가 응답 field 존재/strategy만 검증, decision logic·_GUARD_BLOCK_REFINED·사고흐름 미검증

mandated_18_findings.required_action: core 오염제거·avoid 실배선·영속메모리·memory/commerce_metadata 필드·LLM guardrail 복원 후 연결
mandated_18_findings.evidence: memory contract 빈약·commerce_metadata 없음·개인안전 필드 없음 → '기억하는 커머스' 불가

full_findings.category: Guardrail/Classification: LLM Semantic vs Keyword Heuristic
full_findings.required_action: Restore LLM semantic classification (0-temp) in Foundation guardrail_classify() instead of substring keyword matching (_GR_INJECTION, _GR_INTERNAL, etc.)
full_findings.evidence: Original guardrail_classify (line 706) explicitly states '★휴리스틱 ❌ — LLM이 *의미*로 분류' with structured JSON output {domain:on|off, risk:none|injection|medical|...}. Foundation downgraded to keyword matching: any(k in n for k in _GR_INJECTION) at line 677-678, violating the original 'no heuristics' principle. Original uses _llm('classify') with 0 temperature; Foundation uses substring matching.
full_findings.item: guardrail_classify() implementation - Domain/Risk determination

full_findings.current_status: core.py:692,705; profiles.py:29-32; consult_chat() at 1014-1047
full_findings.required_action: Implement user memory fact loading in judge/consult_chat to retrieve avoid_ingredient/allergy facts and pass them as avoid_atoms to verify_output(). Currently verify_output() signature accepts avoid_atoms (line 692) but NEVER receives it in any call (lines 840, 1021, 1031).
full_findings.evidence: Original: avoid_list(user_id) retrieves DB facts (memory_fact table) filtered by (avoid_ingredient|allergy) and type='active', passed to verify_output() at line 928 and 939. System prompt explicitly includes this list at lines 430-433. Original also has _pitch_variant() (line 1000-1005) that checks product atoms against user avoid_atoms to block recommendations. Foundation: Zero references to memory_fact, active_facts, user_id, or avoid_ingredient in core.py, deepseek_composer.py, or retrieval_provider.py. verify_output() parameter avoid_atoms=None never gets populated. This breaks personalized safety enforcement for users with recorded allergies/ingredient sensitivities.
full_findings.item: Personalized avoid_ingredient/allergy suppression from user memory facts

full_findings.current_status: core.py (none); consult_chat:983-995 (session-only)
full_findings.required_action: Implement persistent user memory fact database (SQLite table equivalent to memory_fact with type/value/confidence/safety_asymmetry). Foundation uses only session-based transient state; cannot remember user facts across sessions.
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py: SQLite schema at lines 83-85 (user, episode, memory_fact, fact_type_registry tables). upsert_fact() (lines 214-284) stores facts with: (1) safety asymmetry - avoid_ingredient/allergy/pregnancy_nursing never decay (line 193-194); (2) confidence gaming - 2-turn rule for non-safety facts (line 248-264); (3) type normalization via CANON whitelist (line 153-163). Foundation: profiles.py line 31 defines empty 'canon': {} slot for injection. core.py has zero database operations. consult_chat() receives session_context from payload and updates it in-memory only (lines 984-992). Memory is never persisted — cannot remember 'I'm allergic to retinol' across conversations.
full_findings.item: User memory database (persistent facts extraction and retrieval)

full_findings.required_action: Document and test that Foundation's keyword-based risk signals (_kw_risk at lines 95-101) are augmented by user memory facts (avoid_ingredient). Currently risk is computed from query text only; user memory should elevate risk level.
full_findings.evidence: Original: guardrail_check() is called before extraction, so it filters at input level. Memory facts are used separately in system_prompt (lines 430-433) and output verification (line 928). Foundation: Risk is computed from normalized query at line 742 (_kw_risk(norm)) without consulting user facts. At line 750, if risk=='high', dt='do_not_buy' and sg='block' regardless of user history. Query 'what retinol should I use' triggers high risk from keyword match; if user has 'avoid_ingredient:retinol' in their facts, Foundation doesn't know.
full_findings.item: Risk determination scope (does it include user memory facts?)

full_findings.category: Output Verification: LLM vs Regex Pattern
full_findings.current_status: core.py:692-712
full_findings.required_action: Integrate avoid_atoms parameter in all verify_output() calls (currently 3 calls at lines 840, 1021, 1031 never pass it). Test that avoid_violation detection works end-to-end.
full_findings.evidence: Original: verify_output() (line 748) uses LLM (0-temp) to detect internal_code_leak, efficacy_overclaim, avoid_violation. Called with avoid_list(user_id) at line 928. Fail-closed: if not v['ok'], attempts LLM correction (lines 933-935), then generic fallback. Foundation: verify_output() at line 692 has avoid_atoms parameter but signature shows None default. Called at lines 840, 1021, 1031 with NO avoid_atoms argument. avoid_atoms parameter is never populated with user facts. Output verification logic is regex-based (_VERIFY_LEAK pattern, _VERIFY_OVERCLAIM keyword list) instead of LLM semantic. More precise patterns but no user personalization.
full_findings.item: verify_output() enforcement for internal leaks, efficacy overclaims, avoid violations

full_findings.required_action: None; both systems block injection (high guardrail_risk) and handle off-domain gracefully.
full_findings.evidence: Original: guardrail_check() returns {block:True} for injection + _RISKS (line 738), returns {block:False} for off-domain with kind='offdomain' (line 745) — allowlist philosophy mentioned in comment (lines 742-743). Foundation: guardrail_classify() returns guardrail_action='deny' for injection/internal (line 678), 'clarify' for off-domain (line 686). Both respect the principle that off-domain is not blocked, only gently redirected.
full_findings.item: Hard-block (injection) vs soft-reject (off-domain allowlist philosophy)

full_findings.current_status: core.py (none); deepseek_composer.py (Korean-only)
full_findings.required_action: Foundation v1 is Korean-only stub. If multilingual support is required, port SIASIU's _detect_lang() (line 464-482), _output_lang() (line 505-514), _localize() (line 399-416), and native greeting generation (line 356-359) to Foundation.
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py: _SUPPORTED_LANGS = 7 (line 485). _detect_lang() uses character ratio (Hangul/Hanzi/Kana/Latin/Thai) with no ML model (line 464-482). _output_lang() determines output language based on system_lang setting + script dominance (line 505-514). _localize() translates prompts via LLM (line 399-416). CLIR at line 540-556 translates queries. _greet_native() generates native-language greetings (line 321-336). Foundation: deepseek_composer.py line 20-27 is Korean-only. profiles.py has no language fields. No CLIR, no script detection, no native greeting generation.
full_findings.item: Multilingual support (7 languages: ko/en/zh/ja/th/vi/ms)

full_findings.current_status: core.py:659-664
full_findings.required_action: Foundation covers: prompt_injection, internal_algorithm_probe, payment_or_account, politics_or_unsafe, off_domain. Missing: emergency (medical 911), minor (age check), pii (other people's info), finance (investment). If required, add _GR_EMERGENCY, _GR_MINOR, _GR_PII, _GR_FINANCE keyword lists.
full_findings.evidence: Original _RISKS tuple (line 654): (injection, medical, finance, politics, payment, emergency, minor, pii). Foundation _GR_* lists at lines 660-664: (injection, internal, payment, politics, offdomain). Emergency (호흡곤란) is in _HIGH list (line 54) but not separated as guardrail_action. Minor (age) has no detection. PII (other person's SSN) not checked. This is partial coverage acceptable for v1.
full_findings.item: Risk categories coverage (injection/medical/finance/politics/payment/emergency/minor/pii)

full_findings.current_status: core.py:117-149 (_classify_intent via keywords)
full_findings.required_action: Foundation's _classify_intent() is keyword-based (greeting/compare/toppick/broad/unclear/category/concern). This is intentional downstream refinement (not replacing guardrail_classify). No action needed if guardrail_classify is fixed to use LLM.
full_findings.evidence: Original guardrail_classify uses LLM to output {domain:on|off, risk:...}. Foundation has separate _classify_intent() (line 117-149) as part of judge pipeline, using keywords against _GREETING/_COMPARE/_TOPPICK etc. This is downstream intent classification for routing, not a replacement for guardrail semantic check. But the original guardrail_classify at line 705-726 was semantic LLM-based; Foundation's guardrail_classify (line 674-689) is keyword-based. That's the regression.
full_findings.item: _classify_intent() semantic classification depth

full_findings.current_status: Completely absent — session-only in-memory dict
full_findings.required_action: Implement persistent memory database layer with episode and memory_fact table schema matching original design
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 16,75-105: `DB_PATH = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, "memory.db")` with tables `episode(id, user_id, ts, role, text)` and `memory_fact(id, user_id, type, value, norm_value, note, confidence, as_of, status, fact_state, source_episode_id, created_at, updated_at)`. Foundation core.py has no DB layer — only ephemeral `session_context` dict (line 975, 1013) with `session_out.get("stated_concerns")` (line 985-989).
full_findings.item: Persistent memory database (SQLite memory.db with episode and memory_fact tables)

full_findings.current_status: Completely absent — no extraction mechanism
full_findings.required_action: Implement fact extraction pipeline that calls LLM to semantically extract durable facts (type, value, confidence) from user messages and persist via upsert_fact()
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 842-861: `_deepseek_extract(m)` calls LLM with system prompt requiring JSON extraction of facts with type, value, note, confidence. Line 855-861: `extract(message, use_deepseek)` returns [(type, value, note, confidence), ...]. Line 966: After every user message, `for (rt, val, note, conf) in extract(message, used): upsert_fact(user_id, rt, val, note, conf, user_eid)`. Foundation has no extraction—the golden test (line 79) only checks memory_recall from passed-in session context, no automatic extraction.
full_findings.item: Fact extraction from user messages (_deepseek_extract + extract functions)

full_findings.foundation_file: Completely absent — no fact lifecycle management
full_findings.current_status: Completely absent — no fact lifecycle management
full_findings.required_action: Implement upsert_fact() with confidence tracking, hypothesis→active promotion on 2nd confirmation (CONF_PROMOTE=0.60), and transactional read-modify-write safety
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 214-284: `upsert_fact(user_id, raw_type, value, note="", confidence=0.6, source_eid=None)` implements: (1) normalize_type validation (line 219-222), (2) existing fact lookup (line 232-234), (3) safety-type fast-path with immediate active promotion (line 237-246), (4) 2-rule gating for non-safety (line 248-264 with CONF_PROMOTE=0.60 threshold on line 54), (5) transactional BEGIN IMMEDIATE (line 230). Foundation has no equivalent—session_context.stated_concerns is a simple list append (core.py lines 985-989) with no confidence, no promotion logic, no persistence.
full_findings.item: Fact persistence with confidence gating and 2-rule promotion (upsert_fact + CONF_PROMOTE)

full_findings.source_function: Canonical fact type registry with immuta
full_findings.foundation_file: Partial/fragmented — hardcoded keywords, no unified registry
full_findings.current_status: Partial/fragmented — hardcoded keywords, no unified registry
full_findings.required_action: Establish unified CANON registry as single source of truth with canonical types (avoid_ingredient, allergy, pregnancy_nursing, skin_type, concern, etc.), aliases, and is_safety flag. Derive SAFETY_TYPES as immutable view of safety-flagged types.
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 35-53: CANON = {"avoid_ingredient": ("회피 성분", 1, [...aliases...]), ...} with 11 fact types. Line 48: SINGLE = {single-valued types}. Line 53: SAFETY_TYPES = {t for t, (_l, _s, _a) in CANON.items() if _s} → {avoid_ingredient, allergy, pregnancy_nursing}. Line 154-163: normalize_type() enforces whitelist only from CANON (default-deny). Foundation core.py lines 168-176 hardcodes _HIGH/_MED/risk_tag_ko in dict, not unified CANON. No type registry, no aliases canonicalization, no principled extensibility.
full_findings.item: Canonical fact type registry with immutability guarantees (CANON dict + SAFETY_TYPES)

full_findings.foundation_file: No immutability guarantee — safety logic fragmented
full_findings.current_status: No immutability guarantee — safety logic fragmented
full_findings.required_action: Enforce immutability of SAFETY_TYPES facts: (1) no supersede of safety facts, (2) no decay/stale-ness, (3) safety facts promoted immediately to active status with confidence ≥0.9, (4) confidence not adjusted downward
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py line 237-246: if safety (from SAFETY_TYPES), insert immediately with active status and confidence=max(confidence, 0.9). Lines 268-272: for non-safety single types, supersede old active facts; for safety types, never supersede. Line 54-56: STALE_DAYS applies only to non-safety facts (line 191-203 _is_stale checks `if fact_row.get("type") in SAFETY_TYPES: return False`). Foundation has no equivalent immutability enforcement. Brief['memory'] (core.py line 1013) treats all stated_concerns equally. Safety-critical facts (pregnancy, allergies) have no stronger persistence guarantee than preferences.
full_findings.item: Safety type immutability constraint (no supersede/decay for avoid_ingredient, allergy, pregnancy_nursing)

full_findings.foundation_file: Absent — no temporal metadata, no weakening
full_findings.current_status: Absent — no temporal metadata, no weakening
full_findings.required_action: Implement time-based staleness for non-safety facts: track as_of timestamp, mark facts stale after STALE_DAYS=90, weaken in memory_context output with '(예전)' label
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 56,190-203: STALE_DAYS=90 constant. _is_stale(fact_row) returns False for SAFETY_TYPES, else checks (now-as_of).days > 90. Line 296-298: memory_context() appends '(예전)' suffix for stale facts: `stale = "(예전)" if _is_stale(f) else ""`. Foundation session_context has no timestamp tracking, no stale weakening.
full_findings.item: Staleness tracking for non-safety facts (STALE_DAYS=90, time-based weakening)

full_findings.source_function: Memory context building from active fact
full_findings.foundation_file: Partial — only session_out.stated_concerns, no rich formatting
full_findings.current_status: Partial — only session_out.stated_concerns, no rich formatting
full_findings.required_action: Extend memory representation to build formatted context from persistent active_facts(), including labels, stale markers, safety warnings (⚠회피), not just condition keys
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 289-299: memory_context(user_id) fetches active_facts(user_id), formats each as `label=value(예전)⚠회피` with CANON label lookup, joins with ' · '. Used in _system_prompt (line 420-422) to inject `[이 고객에 대해 아는 것] {memory_context}`. Foundation core.py line 1013: brief["memory"] = list(session_out.get("stated_concerns") or []) — raw condition keys only, no labels, no formatting, session-only.
full_findings.item: Memory context building from active facts (memory_context function)

full_findings.foundation_file: Absent — no greeting generation from memory
full_findings.current_status: Absent — no greeting generation from memory
full_findings.required_action: Implement recall_greeting(user_id, name, seed, system_lang) to generate greeting with selective fact phrase, e.g., 'OOO고민 말씀해 주셨는데 요의 어떠세요?' using _fact_phrase(fact) for chosen recent/fresh fact
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 338-363: recall_greeting() fetches active_facts(), separates fresh (non-stale) from stale, picks one based on preference order (reaction>concern>goal>skin_type), calls GREET_T[seed].format(name=name, fact=_fact_phrase(pick)) or GREET_NEW. Line 308-315: _fact_phrase() maps fact type to narrative: 'concern' → '{v} 고민', 'skin_type' → '{v} 피부', etc. Foundation has no equivalent greeting—server.py and golden test do not call any greeting generation.
full_findings.item: Personalized greeting with memory reference (recall_greeting + _fact_phrase)

full_findings.current_status: Absent — only current session available
full_findings.required_action: Implement recent_episodes(user_id, limit=10) to retrieve past episode (role, text) pairs for context injection into LLM messages
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 123-127: recent_episodes(user_id, limit=10) fetches episode rows WHERE user_id=? ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT ? and returns reversed list. Line 917-921: used in chat() to build msgs = [system_prompt] + [all recent episodes as {role: "user"|"assistant", content: text}, ...]. Foundation has no episode storage, no multi-turn conversation history beyond current session_context.
full_findings.item: Recent episode retrieval for conversation context (recent_episodes function)

full_findings.current_status: Read-only guards available (cosmile_memory_reuse_adapter.py), but no persistent memory to gate
full_findings.required_action: Integrate trust gate checks (consent, cross-customer isolation, deletion/expiry) into persistent memory write path. Currently guards exist but have no memory to protect.
full_findings.evidence: Original SIASIU has memory_trust_gate_m6.py, foundation_customer_memory_consent_guard.py, foundation_cross_customer_isolation_guard.py, foundation_deleted_expired_memory_guard.py. Foundation-control/cosmile_loop/cosmile_memory_reuse_adapter.py (lines 15-46) calls these guards read-only on incoming memory_record, decide(request_id, memory_record, memory_context). But Foundation core.py has no persistent memory to gate—session_context is ephemeral and passed through without gating. Foundation core.py line 1072: memory_write_performed = False (always).
full_findings.item: Trust gate / cross-customer memory isolation (memory_trust_gate_m6.py + guards)

full_findings.source_function: Composition of answer with memory contex
full_findings.foundation_file: Partial — memory passed but only session-only, no persistent context injection
full_findings.current_status: Partial — memory passed but only session-only, no persistent context injection
full_findings.required_action: Ensure memory context injection uses active_facts() from persistent DB, not just session_out.stated_concerns. Use memory_context() formatted output in _system_prompt.
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py lines 866-967: chat(user_id, message, name, system_lang) calls memory_context(user_id) (line 420), injects into _system_prompt via '[이 고객에 대해 아는 것] {memory_context}' (line 422), then builds msgs with recent_episodes (lines 917-921). Foundation core.py consult_chat calls _build_brief (line 1012) with brief["memory"] = session_out.get("stated_concerns") (line 1013), not persistent memory_context().
full_findings.item: Composition of answer with memory context + grounding (chat function signature)

full_findings.source_function: Risk of fabrication: DeepSeek memory inp
full_findings.foundation_file: Critical gap — DeepSeek receives brief['memory'] as session-only but interprets as historical
full_findings.current_status: Critical gap — DeepSeek receives brief['memory'] as session-only but interprets as historical
full_findings.required_action: Clarify brief['memory'] schema: is it session-only or persistent? If session-only, mark explicitly in LLM prompt to prevent DeepSeek from fabricating continuity. If persistent, implement persistent storage.
full_findings.evidence: Foundation deepseek_composer.py line 63: `memory(이전 대화 고민·참조용·있으면): %s` (previous turn concerns, reference only, if exists). Line 23: `⑤없는 사실·기억 지어내기` (forbidden: fabricating non-existent facts/memories). But Foundation core.py line 1013 passes brief["memory"] = list(session_out.get("stated_concerns") or []), which are detected in the current turn (lines 985-989). DeepSeek receives ["barrier", "sensitivity"] and doesn't know if these were stated in past turns or current turn. Risk: DeepSeek may interpret brief['memory'] as historical facts and reference them as if user said them before, violating the 'no fabrication' constraint.
full_findings.item: Risk of fabrication: DeepSeek memory input interpretation (undefined boundary between session-only and persistent)

full_findings.current_status: retrieval_provider.py line 58: BM25-ONLY. No vector channel at all.
full_findings.required_action: Implement vector.search(query) using e5 embeddings for semantic search channel. Currently retrieval is keyword-only.
full_findings.evidence: SIASIU vector.py:39-46 uses `embs @ embed_query(query)` for semantic similarity. Foundation v1 retrieval_provider.py line 58 only calls `bm25.search(_STATE["idx"], query, k=max(k, 4))` — no vector indexing or search method exists.
full_findings.item: Vector Search (multilingual-e5-small embeddings)

full_findings.current_status: No graph module loaded or referenced. retrieval_provider.py never loads edges table.
full_findings.required_action: Load edges table from ssbrain.sqlite and implement graph.related(atoms) to boost related products based on ingredient/concern atoms. Currently only BM25 chunking.
full_findings.evidence: Original engine.py lines 130-140: `for a in m["atoms"]: for (p, ordn) in self.ing_prod.get(sl, []): fused[p] = fused.get(p, 0.0) + gbase * (1.0 / (1.0 + 0.25 * ordn))`. Foundation has zero graph loading or relationship traversal.
full_findings.item: Graph Relationship Discovery (ingredient→product, concern→product edges)

full_findings.current_status: Not called. No rerank module imported. retrieval_provider.py returns raw BM25 hits without reranking.
full_findings.required_action: Integrate BGE cross-encoder reranking for top-k hits precision improvement. Currently conditional (rerank_on=False by default) but available in v1.
full_findings.evidence: Original engine.py line 188: `from . import rerank; out = rerank.rerank(query, out, top_n=min(20, len(out)))`. Foundation retrieval_provider returns hits at line 66 with no reranking step.
full_findings.item: Reranking (BGE cross-encoder v2-m3 conditional reranking)

full_findings.current_status: Not called. retrieval_provider.py passes raw query to BM25 without language expansion.
full_findings.required_action: Implement bridge.massage(query) CLIR: expand query with multilingual surface forms (e.g., '모공'→['모공','毛孔','pore',...]) before BM25 search for Chinese product KB coverage.
full_findings.evidence: Original bridge.py:46-63 defines BRIDGE with cross-lingual terms. engine.py line 115: `m = bridge.massage(query)` expands query to `q_bm = (query + " " + m["expanded"])`. Foundation retrieval line 58 passes `query` directly without expansion.
full_findings.item: Bridge CLIR Query Expansion (Korean↔English↔Chinese multi-language expansion)

full_findings.foundation_file: No RRF in Foundation v1. Only single BM25 channel, no fusion.
full_findings.current_status: No RRF in Foundation v1. Only single BM25 channel, no fusion.
full_findings.required_action: Extend retrieval to return both BM25 and vector channels, fuse with RRF(channels, k=60). Currently retrieval_provider returns single channel only.
full_findings.evidence: Original engine.py:39-45 implements RRF scoring: `scores[doc_id] = scores.get(doc_id, 0.0) + 1.0 / (k + rank + 1)`. Line 124: `fused = rrf(_ch) if _ch else {}` fuses BM25+vector. Foundation has no equivalent.
full_findings.item: RRF (Reciprocal Rank Fusion) Multi-Channel Fusion

full_findings.foundation_file: Not implemented. retrieval_provider skips name indexing entirely.
full_findings.current_status: Not implemented. retrieval_provider skips name indexing entirely.
full_findings.required_action: Load ingredient/product name indices (name2doc, prod_name2doc) from SQLite and boost exact matches +NAME_BOOST. Currently all terms treated equally in BM25.
full_findings.evidence: Original engine.py lines 144-153: `if qn in self.name2doc: nd = self.name2doc[qn]; fused[nd] = fused.get(nd, 0.0) + NAME_BOOST` boosts exact ingredient matches. Foundation has no name index.
full_findings.item: Name Matching Boosts (exact ingredient/product name normalization)

full_findings.current_status: core.py line 67 has _CATEGORY_KW but retrieval doesn't use it to boost product types.
full_findings.required_action: Extract product types from query (토너→toner, 크림→cream) and boost matching product doc_ids in retrieval scoring.
full_findings.evidence: Original engine.py lines 154-158: `qtypes = [en for ko, en in TYPE_MAP.items() if ko in ql]; if qtypes: for _d, _m in self.docs.items(): if _m.get("doc_type") == "product" and any(t in _d for t in qtypes): fused[_d] = fused.get(_d, 0.0) + 0.35`. Foundation has keyword list but no retrieval integration.
full_findings.item: Product Type Detection & Boosting (토너/크림/세럼 discrimination)

full_findings.current_status: Not referenced in core.py or retrieval_provider.py. _FAMILY_GUIDANCE replaces with hardcoded templates.
full_findings.required_action: Inject SEED_KB as fallback knowledge when KB retrieval is empty. Currently all answers synthesized from hardcoded _FAMILY_GUIDANCE.
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py defines SEED_KB with structured facts (key/answer pairs). Foundation core.py _FAMILY_GUIDANCE (lines 885-907) has no connection to SEED_KB and uses only template principle/avoid/direction.
full_findings.item: SEED_KB (General Skin Care Knowledge Base)

full_findings.current_status: Not referenced. core.py defines safety rules inline without CANON structure.
full_findings.required_action: Use CANON taxonomy (avoid_ingredient, allergy, pregnancy_nursing as safety-critical) instead of hardcoded _HIGH/_MED keyword lists in core.py:53-58.
full_findings.evidence: Original brain.py line 53: `SAFETY_TYPES = {t for t, (_l, _s, _a) in CANON.items() if _s}` extracts safety-critical types from registry. Foundation core.py line 53-58 hardcodes _HIGH/_MED keywords with no structured registry.
full_findings.item: CANON Fact Type Registry & SAFETY_TYPES

full_findings.foundation_file: Not loaded. retrieval_provider never loads product_ingredients table.
full_findings.current_status: Not loaded. retrieval_provider never loads product_ingredients table.
full_findings.required_action: Load product_ingredients table to enable ingredient→product and product→ingredient lookups for context-aware filtering. Currently no ingredient-product relationships in retrieval.
full_findings.evidence: Original engine.py lines 100-106 load ing_prod (ingredient→products) and prod_ings (product→ingredients) from database. Foundation retrieval_provider never touches product_ingredients table.
full_findings.item: Ingredient/Product Registry Linking (ing_prod, prod_ings mappings)

full_findings.current_status: core.py defines _ANSWER_TYPE dictionary (lines 502-510) but classifier not called. Only intent-based type assignment.
full_findings.required_action: Use answer_type_classifier.classify(query) to override answer_type from refined_intent. Currently types are static mappings without query analysis.
full_findings.evidence: Original answer_type_classifier.py lines 32-39 classify(query, hits=None) returns {answer_type, reason, high_risk} with 15+ types (pregnancy_lactation_question, medical_boundary_question, adverse_reaction_question, etc.). Foundation core.py line 861 only maps refined_intent→answer_type statically.
full_findings.item: Answer Type Classifier Integration

full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 885-907: _FAMILY_GUIDANCE with 7 hardcoded families (principle/avoid/direction). Not sourced from KB retrieval.
full_findings.required_action: Treat _FAMILY_GUIDANCE as fallback templates only. Prefer KB-retrieved evidence via consult_chat(compose=true)→retrieval_provider.search()→evidence snippets. Templates should be framework, not primary source.
full_findings.evidence: Foundation core.py lines 885-907 hardcodes _FAMILY_GUIDANCE dictionary with static principle/avoid/direction for each concern family. No connection to SIASIU KB or SEED_KB. Original design intends to inject KB-derived explanations via retrieval + DeepSeek composer, not static templates.
full_findings.item: _FAMILY_GUIDANCE Hardcoded Templates vs KB-Grounded Explanations

full_findings.current_status: retrieval_provider.py line 58: `hits = bm25.search(_STATE["idx"], query, k=max(k, 4))[:k]` returns k=4 BM25 hits. No fallback channels.
full_findings.required_action: v1 minimum search should return BM25 hits ONLY (acceptable baseline). v1.1 MUST integrate vector + graph + RRF for hybrid recall (current design intent).
full_findings.evidence: Original engine.py implements multi-stage retrieval: (1) BM25 + vector via RRF, (2) graph boosts per atom, (3) name/type boosts, (4) optional reranking. Foundation v1 retrieval_provider only step (1) with BM25 channel only. Missing: vector channel, graph boost, name/type boosts, reranking.
full_findings.item: Retrieval Quality & Recall (986 chunks default vs multi-channel hybrid)

full_findings.current_status: core.py implements evidence_mode_decide (line 715-726) but doesn't use hit evidence scores or confidence_model.
full_findings.required_action: Integrate evidence_mode.evidence_answer_mode(hits, query) to compute evidence_score from retrieval hit quality. Currently evidence_mode capped by rule-based confidence, not hit analysis.
full_findings.evidence: Original evidence_mode.py:38-73 computes evidence_answer_mode({mode, source_layer, evidence_score}) from hits + claim_type. Foundation core.py evidence_mode_decide doesn't accept hits, only grounded boolean flag.
full_findings.item: Evidence Mode & Confidence Scoring

full_findings.current_status: core.py uses decision_type (recommend/do_not_recommend/do_not_buy/hold/ask_more/cannot_determine). answer_provenance.py defines answer_mode (assertive/grounded/cautious/uncertain/cannot_determine).
full_findings.required_action: Map decision_type to answer_mode: recommend→grounded, do_not_recommend→cautious, do_not_buy→uncertain, ask_more→uncertain, hold→uncertain, cannot_determine→cannot_determine. Currently concepts are confused.
full_findings.evidence: answer_provenance.py:11 defines ANSWER_MODE = ("assertive", "grounded", "cautious", "uncertain", "cannot_determine"). core.py:39 uses DECISION_TYPES = ("recommend", "do_not_recommend", "do_not_buy", "hold", "ask_more", "cannot_determine"). They should map, not coexist.
full_findings.item: Answer Provenance (answer_mode vs decision_type mismatch)

full_findings.current_status: deepseek_composer.py:20-26
full_findings.required_action: Restore full PERSONA + TALK_STYLE (110 lines) to Foundation composer system prompt
full_findings.evidence: Original: PERSONA(368-381) + TALK_STYLE(383-395) embedded in system prompt. Foundation: _SYS = 7 lines minimal + 6 prohibitions only. Original includes 7 principles (functionality, empathy, emotion, mirroring, firmness, source attribution, language). Foundation has none.
full_findings.item: System Prompt - PERSONA + Instructions

full_findings.current_status: core.py:692-712
full_findings.required_action: Implement actual LLM-based verify_output; current rule patterns miss semantic violations
full_findings.evidence: Original: LLM with system prompt analyzing content semantically for {internal, efficacy, avoid}. Foundation: Regex patterns (_VERIFY_LEAK, _VERIFY_OVERCLAIM) + substring matching. Misses nuanced violations like '효과 있어요' (not in list) vs '완치' (is). Pattern-based = false negatives.
full_findings.item: verify_output function - verification method (LLM vs regex)

full_findings.required_action: Foundation splits issues into 6 types but _VERIFY_MEMORY_CLAIM only applies when !grounded = incomplete fabrication detection
full_findings.evidence: Original 3 types (internal, efficacy, avoid) vs Foundation 6 (internal_code_leak, efficacy_overclaim, disclosure_leak, fabrication, avoid_violation, preg_lact_safety_assertion). Foundation pattern '저장된' only triggers when !grounded (line 703), missing bare memory claims when grounded=true.
full_findings.item: verify_output issue types - coverage and severity mapping

full_findings.required_action: avoid_atoms never populated/passed to verify_output; line 1021 calls verify_output(...avoid_atoms=None) - avoid_violation check is inert
full_findings.evidence: Original: avoid_atoms=_norm_value() via bridge.massage(). Foundation: grounded_gate runs at line 1006 but avoid_atoms never extracted or passed to verify_output(line 1021). Verify_output(avoid_atoms=None) at line 1021 means line 705 check 'if avoid_atoms and any(a in text...)' always False.
full_findings.item: avoid_violation check - substance vs atom matching

full_findings.source_function: enforcement application - when and how s
full_findings.required_action: Original uses interactive retry loop with LLM correction; Foundation just sets answer_text=None then rule_compose
full_findings.evidence: Original (926-943): verify_output fails → _llm('chat', msgs + correction_prompt) → re-verify → if still fails, generic safe response. Foundation (1018-1030): verify_output fails → answer_text=None → _rule_compose(brief). No semantic correction attempted, only template fallback.
full_findings.item: enforcement application - when and how safety fallback runs

full_findings.required_action: None - safety_first suppression works in both
full_findings.evidence: Both correctly suppress products when strategy=safety_first. Foundation line 1039 products=[].
full_findings.item: enforcement_applied logic - safety_first product suppression

full_findings.source_function: persistent fact storage and recall with
full_findings.current_status: core.py:993-995 (session_context only, no fact DB)
full_findings.required_action: Foundation has NO memory_fact table, no norm_value, no confidence tracking, no 2-turn promotion. Session_context=['barrier'] is stateless string, not a typed/persisted fact.
full_findings.evidence: Original: memory_fact(id, user_id, type, value, norm_value, confidence, as_of, status, fact_state) with upsert_fact rule: non-safe facts start as hypothesis, promote to active on 2nd mention (line 256-259). Foundation: session['stated_concerns']=['barrier'] is a string list, no normalization, no promotion rule, no decay.
full_findings.item: persistent fact storage and recall with confidence game

full_findings.source_function: memory context in system prompt - type-t
full_findings.required_action: Foundation passes session['stated_concerns'] as plain strings; original system_prompt includes type labels and safety flags
full_findings.evidence: Original: '[이 고객에 대해 아는 것] 회피성분=레티놀(⚠회피), 고민=여드름, 목표=...' - types and flags visible to LLM. Foundation: 'memory: ["barrier", "sensitivity"]' - no type information, LLM guesses context from words alone.
full_findings.item: memory context in system prompt - type-tagged facts vs plain strings

full_findings.source_function: language detection and mirroring per tur
full_findings.current_status: deepseek_composer.py (no language detection)
full_findings.required_action: Foundation does NOT detect message language or mirror; language fixed at profile level
full_findings.evidence: Original _output_lang(): detects message language via _detect_lang(), mirrors if dominant foreign script present. _greet_native() generates native-language greetings via LLM. Foundation: profile language only, no per-turn mirroring, no native greeting generation.
full_findings.item: language detection and mirroring per turn

full_findings.current_status: core.py:715-726
full_findings.required_action: Foundation implements evidence_mode_decide but lacks confidence_model scoring and source_layer classification
full_findings.evidence: Original: evidence_mode.py calls cm.answer_mode(score, claim, has_tier1=...) returning {mode, source_layer, evidence_score, reason}. Foundation: evidence_mode_decide returns {answer_mode, ...} with no source_layer or evidence_score. No confidence model integration.
full_findings.item: answer mode classification with confidence scoring

full_findings.current_status: deepseek_composer.py (no provenance logic)
full_findings.required_action: Foundation has NO answer_provenance module; composer receives evidence but doesn't select attribution phrases
full_findings.evidence: Original: select_phrase(answer_mode='cautious', source_layer='learned') → '현재 수집·검토된 자료 기준으로는'. User sees source confidence. Foundation: composer gets evidence[] as list; no phrase selection logic. Answers lack source attribution signaling.
full_findings.item: answer provenance phrase selection based on answer_mode and source

full_findings.current_status: retrieval_provider.py:51-69
full_findings.required_action: Foundation BM25 retrieval lacks CLIR (cross-lingual query translation) and avoid_docs filtering
full_findings.evidence: Original kb_search: CLIR via _translate_query(), avoid_docs via _graph.related(ing_atoms), RRF fusion + rerank. Foundation: bm25.search() only, no cross-lingual variants, no avoid filtering at retrieval stage.
full_findings.item: kb_search with CLIR, avoid_docs filtering, and reranking

full_findings.current_status: core.py:990-991, session_context tracking
full_findings.required_action: Foundation correctly tracks recommendation_deferred flag but lacks typed memory of *why* (no 'goal' type fact). Case 11 context degraded without fact_type information.
full_findings.evidence: Golden case 9-10: recommendation_deferred=True maintained across turns, products=0 enforced. Correct. BUT case 11 composer has only session['stated_concerns']=['barrier'] - no explicit fact like type='goal' value='barrier_education_priority'. LLM must infer from string label alone.
full_findings.item: deferred recommendation + serum follow-up context maintenance

full_findings.current_status: core.py:666, 697
full_findings.required_action: Foundation has _VERIFY_LEAK regex only; missing full disclosure_policy module for {customer,partner,investor,internal} audience-based redaction
full_findings.evidence: Original: classify_sensitivity(text) checks against {scoring_formula, source_weight, reranker_routing_rule, ...}. Foundation: _VERIFY_LEAK only checks for 'RISK_', 'do_not_buy', 'decisionType'. Misses broader disclosure categories like 'embedding model', 'source weight', 'tier threshold'.
full_findings.item: never_disclose pattern classification and external message redaction

full_findings.current_status: core.py:117-241
full_findings.required_action: Different architecture; Foundation uses intent_type + risk gates instead of answer_type rules. Functional outcome equivalent but structure differs.
full_findings.evidence: Original answer_type_classifier returns {answer_type, high_risk} from rules. Foundation _classify_intent returns intent_type, then judge() applies high-risk gates (line 687-689). Same blocking behavior, different code path.
full_findings.item: answer_type classification with HIGH_RISK mapping

full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 67-68: _CATEGORY_KW = {cream, serum, toner, essence, cleanser, suncare}; lines 153-162: _CONDITION_KW = {dryness, barrier, sensitivity, wrinkle, pigmentation, pore, acne, oiliness}
full_findings.required_action: Extract _CATEGORY_KW, _CONDITION_KW, _COND_LABEL, _CATEGORY_LABEL from core.py to profile injection. Current _pget() usage only at line 108; other 30+ constant references bypass profile injection entirely.
full_findings.evidence: core.py line 67-68 hardcodes product form types. Line 154: ('건조', '보습', '당김') for dryness condition. profiles.py line 17-20 provides injection slot but core.py ignores it in most functions. Cannot be reused for non-beauty verticals.
full_findings.item: K-beauty specific vocabulary hardcoded instead of injected

full_findings.category: Dim 11: Core Contamination - Concern Family Models
full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 197-240: _FAMILY_MODEL[8 families] with explain_intro/clarify_axes; lines 885-907: _FAMILY_GUIDANCE[8 families] with skincare principles/avoidance/direction
full_findings.required_action: Move _FAMILY_MODEL and _FAMILY_GUIDANCE from core.py to profiles.py as injected concern_family_model and family_guidance_db. These are completely domain-specific and prevent code reuse.
full_findings.evidence: Line 199: _FAMILY_MODEL['acne_or_trouble']['explain_intro'] = 'yeodeureum pibu-neun piji, mogong maghim, yeom-jeung, pibu jangbyeok sangtae-reul hamkke boneun ge joa-yo.' (skincare-specific). Line 886: '민감·자극 피부는 장벽이 약해 자극에 예민해요...' (cosmetic tissue physiology specific)
full_findings.item: Cosmetic-specific educational guidance hardcoded in _FAMILY_MODEL and _FAMILY_GUIDANCE

full_findings.category: Dim 11: Core Contamination - Hardcoded Response Strings
full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 336, 342, 345, 755-783, 827-831, 961 contain 10+ hardcoded Korean strings mentioning 피부 고민/제품/성분
full_findings.required_action: Extract all user-facing strings from judge() and _plain_explanation() to profile message_templates. Examples: line 336 '안녕하세요. 피부 고민이나 찾으시는 제품을...', line 961 '내부 정책상 그 요청은 도와드리기 어려워요. 피부 고민이나 제품·성분에...'
full_findings.evidence: Line 336-345: Three _plain_explanation() branches return domain-specific strings. Line 755-783: judge() decision tree returns clarification_questions with skincare terminology (건조/장벽/유분/토너/크림). No profile injection point exists. Requires code edit to change for different domain.
full_findings.item: Skincare-specific fallback messages and ask-more prompts embedded throughout judge()

full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 53-59: _HIGH and _MED contain cosmetic actives (레티놀, 스테로이드) and symptoms (화끈, 부어올라, 두드러기); lines 166-171 hardcode _RISK_TAG mappings
full_findings.required_action: Move _HIGH, _MED, _SAFETY_KW, _RISK_TAG, _RISK_TAG_KO to profiles.py under safety_keywords_high/medium and risk_tag_mappings. Core should use generic decision logic (risk_level = max(structured_signal, keyword_risk)) without hardcoded lexicons.
full_findings.evidence: Lines 53-57: _HIGH includes ingredient names (레티놀, 스테로이드) specific to skincare domain. Line 156: ('따가', '예민', '민감', '자극') are cosmetic-specific symptom keywords that don't apply to non-beauty domains like investment advice or fitness.
full_findings.item: K-beauty specific symptom keywords in safety gate decision logic

full_findings.category: Dim 11: Core Contamination - Intent Classification Keywords
full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 61-73: _GREETING, _COMPARE, _TOPPICK, _BROAD, _UNCLEAR, _PURCHASE, _ROUTINE, _USAGE_EDU, _ADVERSE_SOFT contain product-form keywords (토너/크림/세럼/피부 고민)
full_findings.required_action: Move keyword detection lists (_GREETING, _COMPARE, _ROUTINE, _USAGE_EDU, _ADVERSE_SOFT, _CATEGORY_KW) to profile as intent_keyword_lexicon. Core should provide generic framework without domain assumptions.
full_findings.evidence: Line 62: _COMPARE includes '좋아 크림', '좋아 세럼' (product-form specific comparisons). Line 70: _ROUTINE = ('순서', '루틴', '언제 발라', '바르는 법') are skincare-specific routine markers. Intent logic is reusable; keywords should be profiled.
full_findings.item: Cosmetic-domain keyword lists hardcoded in intent classification functions

full_findings.category: Dim 12: API Response Contract - Missing Commerce Fields
full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 1050-1096: consult_chat() response includes request_id, answer, decision_type, final_product_count, recommended_products, trace but NO: product_availability, brand_metadata, shopping_context, price_range, inventory_status
full_findings.required_action: Add optional response fields for commerce: under 'commerce_metadata' (if service_profile=='cosmile'): product_availability_status, shopping_eligible, brand_context, inventory_hint. These are additive, do not break existing contract.
full_findings.evidence: consult_chat() response structure complete for judgment/answer. Cosmile integration requires additional context: whether products are purchasable, brand positioning, inventory status. These can be added as optional fields in response without breaking API.
full_findings.item: consult_chat() response lacks commerce-specific metadata for Cosmile integration

full_findings.category: Dim 12: API Response Contract - Session State Sufficiency
full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 984-995: session_context_out only tracks stated_concerns (array), recommendation_deferred (bool), last_refined_intent; memory_recall returns stated_concerns only
full_findings.required_action: Document session_context and memory_recall contract completeness. If richer state tracking needed (user_preferences, avoid_ingredient atoms, persona), expand session_context schema. Current minimal session sufficient for MVP but may need enrichment for long-term retention.
full_findings.evidence: SIASIU tracks 10+ fact types; Foundation v1 tracks 2 (stated_concerns, recommendation_deferred). Sufficient for single session but multi-session personalization limited. API contract is clear; enhancement is v1.1 work.
full_findings.item: Memory and session context implementation may be incomplete for rich multi-turn scenarios

full_findings.category: Dim 13: Test Quality - Field Validation vs Logic Validation
full_findings.source_file: No published test baseline in SIASIU; app tested via integration elsewhere
full_findings.source_function: Golden test validates response field pre
full_findings.current_status: foundation_brain_v1_golden_test.py lines 34-87: 20 test cases check exp_strategy, final_product_count, retrieval/deepseek calls, and leak detection. NO validation of: condition detection accuracy, intent classification correctness, guard_bl
full_findings.required_action: Upgrade test coverage: (1) Add condition detection assertions: '제가 자극이 있어' must detect sensitivity condition. (2) Add intent classification assertions: '크림과 세럼 뭐가 나아?' must classify as product_type_comparison not product_recommendation. (3) Add guard_block assertions: candidate presence must NOT override non-product intents (skin_concern_explanation stays explain, not recommend). (4) Add 8+ adversarial cases: jailbreak, off-domain, ambiguous safety.
full_findings.evidence: Test line 60: base('01_sensitive_skin', chat('민감피부에 어떤 화장품이 좋지?'), 'answer_then_clarify', 0, ...) only asserts answer_strategy field. No assertions that: (a) conditions detected correctly, (b) intent classified correctly, (c) concern_family populated correctly, (d) no products returned despite candidates. Test is 'field-passing' not 'logic validation'.
full_findings.item: Golden test validates response field presence/strategy but not decision logic correctness

full_findings.category: Dim 13: Test Quality - Missing Guard Block Coverage
full_findings.source_file: SIASIU has no comparable guard block logic; answer type is simple enum
full_findings.current_status: core.py lines 495-500 define _GUARD_BLOCK_REFINED frozenset with 10 refined intents that should NOT convert to recommend even with candidates. lines 798-804 downgrade decision if guard triggered. Test does NOT cover this.
full_findings.required_action: Add mandatory test coverage for each _GUARD_BLOCK_REFINED case: (1) skin_concern_explanation with candidates -> ask_more (not recommend). (2) education_request with candidates -> ask_more. (3) comparison_question with candidates -> ask_more. (4) routine_guidance with candidates -> ask_more. (5) Verify decision_type downgraded from recommend to ask_more when refined_intent in guard set.
full_findings.evidence: Test line 39-58 only checks answer_strategy matches expected value. Core.py lines 798-804 implement critical logic: 'if dt in (recommend, do_not_recommend) and refined_intent in _GUARD_BLOCK_REFINED: dt = ask_more'. No golden test verifies this downgrade occurs. Example: if chat returns recommend but refined_intent=skin_concern_explanation, decision MUST downgrade to ask_more; test doesn't check this.
full_findings.item: Golden test does not validate _GUARD_BLOCK_REFINED logic prevents recommendation override

full_findings.category: Dim 13: Test Quality - Compositional Output Validation
full_findings.source_function: Test validates leak patterns but not ans
full_findings.current_status: foundation_brain_v1_golden_test.py lines 48-49: only regex-checks for leaks (RISK_* keywords, internal codes). Does NOT validate: answer tone matches strategy (safety_first -> cautious language), answer contains next_questions, answer menti
full_findings.required_action: Add output content validation: (1) When answer_strategy='safety_first', answer must contain caution markers (피하다/주의/확인/전문가/상담). (2) When answer_strategy='clarify_first', next_questions non-empty. (3) When answer_strategy='recommend_with_caution', answer mentions product AND includes caution. (4) When evidence=empty, answer must contain uncertainty markers (확실치/어려워/따라다를/예상).
full_findings.evidence: Test line 48 checks LEAK regex but not content quality. Example: test 02_tingle_recommend_safety expects answer_strategy='safety_first' but only checks answer doesn't contain 'RISK_*' keywords. Doesn't verify answer actually says something like '전문가 상담이 먼저' or '피해야 할' (which DeepSeek should compose, but rule fallback might skip).
full_findings.item: Test validates leak patterns but not answer content alignment with strategy/context

full_findings.category: Dim 13: Test Quality - Session/Memory Multi-Turn Chains
full_findings.current_status: foundation_brain_v1_golden_test.py lines 71-79: tests 09_defer_set and 10_defer_maintained check recommendation_deferred flag persists, and memory_recall matches stated_concerns. Does NOT validate: (1) memory correctly composed into compose
full_findings.required_action: Add multi-turn session chains: (1) 3-turn scenario: concern stated -> recommendation deferred -> product query -> verify deferred suppresses products across all 3 turns with enforcement. (2) Memory accumulation: 2 concerns in turn 1, 1 new concern in turn 2 -> verify session_context_out.stated_concerns == {concern1, concern2, concern3}. (3) Verify memory passed to composer: check brief['memory'] == session_context_out.stated_concerns in trace.
full_findings.evidence: Lines 1013, 1063: core.py passes memory to composer via brief['memory'] and returns memory_recall in response. Test doesn't verify: (a) memory correctly received by composer, (b) multiple concerns accumulate, (c) accumulation persists across turns. Test only checks flag on single turn.
full_findings.item: Session persistence and memory composition tested superficially; no dependency chains
